$schema: see scripts/lanes.mjs — `node scripts/lanes.mjs check` validates this file
$why: Four agents, four checkouts, one repo. The prose ledger in ade-coordination.md records intent; this file records ownership in a form a script can reject. Both exist on purpose: prose carries the reasoning, this carries the constraint.
$shared: Globs every lane may edit, because coordination itself lives in them. Append, never rewrite: a lane that overwrites another's entry has destroyed the record that would have shown the collision. package.json is here because every lane adds scripts to it — but a dependency change is still a claim, and belongs in ade-coordination.md first.

lanes.role: everything a person sees — build-status page, its components, navigation
typed selectors and backend libraries — no UI, no data authoring
lanes.$queueNote: Empty as of 2026-07-23 cf13087, and honestly so. agent-config-persistence needs a new Prisma model and a migration against the one Postgres all four checkouts share; packages/db is not this lane and the repo's rules say schema changes are one writer at a time, so it waits for a human rather than being run unattended. Its other half — 'or give them an explicit disabled state' — is already satisfied (agent-config-drawer.tsx renders a disabled Save and a 'Not saved yet' badge). live-throughput-metrics is blocked on the internal-token lifecycle decision and needs an Overview tile, which is not this lane either. Next: tests for scripts/lanes.mjs, which enforces discipline on four agents and has no test of its own — its error paths have only ever been proven by hand against a scratch fixture.

lanes.role: the record itself and the path that writes it — tracker JSON, API route, editor
lanes.$queueNote: silence-a-finding was removed on 2026-07-23: ridley shipped it in 29a1362 while it sat here. The checker flagged it the moment main was rebased in, which is the whole point — C-data had not started, so nothing was wasted. Pick the next item from `pnpm board`.

lanes.role: drives the running app on localhost and reports what is actually broken — owns no source file on purpose
lanes.$note: Owning nothing is the point. A lane that both builds and signs off on its own work is not verification. It files findings against the tracker and against whichever lane owns the file; it does not fix them.
